text: Oral iron supplementation significantly increases serum ferritin levels in athletes compared to control groups.
Oral iron supplementation does not significantly increase blood haemoglobin levels in athletes.
Oral iron supplementation shows a trend toward a small positive effect on VO2max in young healthy athletes, but the effect is not statistically significant.
Oral iron supplementation is most effective at increasing serum ferritin in athletes with pre-supplementation ferritin levels at or below 12 µg/l, with minimal effect at higher baseline levels.
Effective oral iron supplementation doses for improving hematological parameters in athletes range from 16 to 100 mg of elementary iron daily.
Oral iron supplementation protocols shorter than 6 weeks are ineffective for improving blood iron status in athletes; a minimum duration of 6-8 weeks is needed.
Oral iron supplementation does not significantly alter transferrin saturation or serum transferrin receptor concentration in athletes.
